pattern: The largest odd substring is the whole prefix ending at the rightmost odd digit, so scan from the end for the first odd digit.
hints: An odd integer must end in an odd digit, so the answer ends at some position holding an odd digit.
To maximize the value, keep as many leading digits as possible, so take the longest prefix that ends in an odd digit.
Scan from the right for the first odd digit at index i; return num[0..i] inclusive, or the empty string if none is odd.

edge_inputs.description: trailing even digits must be dropped
two chars, last even, first odd
odd digit in the middle, evens after

stress.description: long random digit string without leading zero
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    first = str(rng.randrange(1, 10))
    rest = ''.join(str(rng.randrange(0, 10)) for _ in range(n - 1))
    return [first + rest]
